input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.4.2.5 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.4.2.5
            TITLE: if an implausibility occurs between the values of the appss and persists for more than 100

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.2.5: If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100
T.4.2.5 If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100 
msec, the power to the (IC) Electronic Throttle / (EV) Motor(s) must be immediately stopped 
completely.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 65 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
(EV only) It is not necessary to Open the Shutdown Circuit, the motor controller(s) stopping 
the power to the Motor(s) is sufficient.

final_answer: If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100 msec, the power to the (IC) Electronic Throttle / (EV) Motor(s) must be immediately stopped completely. (EV only) It is not necessary to Open the Shutdown Circuit, the motor controller(s) stopping the power to the Motor(s) is sufficient.
